essence: At the Saturday AI engineers meetup David and peers dug into Claude Code's new remote-control server, Opus 4.6's 1M-token context, Chrome 146's native MCP exposure of live browsing sessions, and agent supply-chain security — distinguishing prompt injection from dependency-injection attacks via GitHub/NPM/Hugging Face — with mitigations of Bubblewrap/container sandboxing, exhaustive permission allowlists with domain whitelists, and building tools in-house instead of pulling random repos.

actions.text: Include the South Park-style self-documents idea in upcoming video content

essence: Session-close directive to an agent: fix the thumbnail test to use an array/table, generate chapter JSON with any improvements, persist the discussion outputs as documented actions, commit/push everything and verify the repo is clean, then answer the open question of how each app (FliGen as the example) currently gets started.

actions.text: Persist session outputs as actions, commit all changes, and verify no uncommitted files remain

essence: Meetup demo dissected the Paperclip runtime: a parent process streams a prompt to a child agent over stdin and filters typed JSON messages from stdout — fire-and-forget with no interactive back-and-forth (which is much harder to build) — plus PARA/second-brain skill injection and a CEO-approval workflow that auto-creates projects, issues, and todo items unprompted.

Friday-night call with the Philippines team: the Mac purchase (iMac, keyboards, 27-inch Samsung S7 monitor) came in under budget with ~9,000 pesos spare (buy Mary a mouse), setup will start Ansible-first with everything screencast, Mary will pivot AI-TLDR toward agentic-OS content while keeping the graphics niche via Nano Banana infographics, the five machines will be file-synced into one networked 'supercomputer', and the skills will eventually be taught to Vasilios.

actions.text: Email photos of all purchase receipts to David
Schedule and run Ansible-first setup of the new Mac with screencasting (weekend or Monday)
Mary to start building agentic operating system content mixed with graphics

essence: David put his multi-machine problem to the meetup — keeping skills and a 1,500-note second brain identical across three Macs without fragile git commit/pull pulses — surveying Tailscale ad-hoc copies, Postgres-with-vectors skill storage, FreeFileSync, and a continuous peer-to-peer Dropbox-style sync, followed by a Paperclip Docker walkthrough exposing its adapter-pattern stdin/stdout agent spawning, org-chart structure, cost tracking, and fire-and-forget limitation.

Meetup closing reflection: AI tools ship magical-but-low-quality and improve fast (Cursor to Claude Code, OpenClaw to hands-off orchestration platforms in about six weeks), making weekly currency competitively mandatory, and Nick plans a camera/hardware capture rig feeding meetup interactions into his 'ghost' agent system.

Meetup exchange on multi-agent workflows: CrewAI/AutoGen-style delegation to specialized sub-agents works for both coding and business processes, extended thinking and skills now cover much of the old multi-agent value, and OpenAI's Symphony can pick tickets straight off a Linear board — pitched as useful for legacy, non-agent-first companies and simple for a lead engineer to implement.

Fragmented meetup chatter on model selection: route basic data processing to cheaper models, use free Gemini to kick off tasks like slide decks, and build personal home systems on open-source code plus the agents SDK.

Nick's survey of orchestration/control-plane tools (Paperclip, Tmux-based orchestrators, Demux, OpenAI Symphony/swarms with Claude's teammate flag) concluded each merely encodes one developer's opinionated terminal workflow, and spinning up many parallel agents mostly manufactures review overhead — the criterion for useful orchestration is that it reduces user oversight rather than multiplying it.

David articulated the missing product shape: a Kanban board where every task carries a multi-agent hierarchy (orchestrator plus downstream pen-test/unit/E2E/UAT/docs agents), long-running work with human-in-the-loop checkpoints instead of Ralph-loop premature worktree merges, and a unified cross-terminal telemetry/reflection layer that ingests transcripts, hooks, and tool calls to spot repeated prompts and propose skills — the seed spec for AngelEye-style observability across his 20-30 parallel conversations.

actions.text: Vibe-code own telemetry system by pillaging patterns from existing telemetry projects

essence: David laid out his build philosophy at the meetup: AppyStack (Express+React+Socket.io) with 'recipes' prompts that regenerate desktop-like harnesses on demand, every app agentic-first via Swagger APIs plus skills while GUIs remain for deterministic human use, vibe-coded MVPs later hardened through BMAD brownfield integration, and a three-cofounder split where domain expert Angela designs workflows via Mochaccino — plus a new lead to wire Automator/AppleScript into the agentic OS.

actions.text: Research Automator and AppleScript and how Claude Code can drive them; add to the agentic OS design

essence: Before the Agents in the Wild session David live-demoed his OMI pipeline (pendant transcripts pulled from the M4 Mini at home over Tailscale/SSH, structured as JSON and rendered to HTML for research) while mid-project on a vibe-coded bilingual Thai/English menu, AI-generated QR codes, and a CAD-style layout tool for Joy's nail-and-drinks shop — with an open doubt about whether OMI can transcribe Thai.
